content.full_text:
, I I 
, 
I 
Ted Prim 
January II, 1977 
Source of Income 
87103(c) 
-re: 
AG Ops 76-236 requested by Willie Brown 
If Brown's law firm agrees to represent a physician 
in a license revocation hearing but no fees have been re-
ceived by the firm, can the physician be a source of income 
to Brown? 
The staff concluded at a meeting on January 10, 
1977, that the physician could be a source of income to 
Brown if it were probable that Brown's pro rata share of 
the fees would total $250 or more. 
The rationale was that 
the income had been "promised" to the official once services 
had been agreed upon which would probably generate the requi-
site income. 
I advised Floyd of these views on January 1 , 1977. 
TP: jo

sections.question: If Brown's law firm agrees to represent a physician in a license revocation hearing but no fees have been received by the firm, can the physician be a source of income to Brown?
sections.conclusion: The staff concluded at a meeting on January 10, 1977, that the physician could be a source of income to Brown if it were probable that Brown's pro rata share of the fees would total $250 or more. The rationale was that the income had been "promised" to the official once services had been agreed upon which would probably generate the requisite income.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a physician can be considered a source of income to an official when the official's law firm has agreed to represent the physician in a license revocation hearing but has not yet received fees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff concluded that a physician can be a source of income to the official if the official's pro rata share of fees would probably total $250 or more, based on income being "promised" once services are agreed upon that would likely generate the required threshold amount.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks standard section headers but contains identifiable question and conclusion sections. References AG Ops 76-236 and Willie Brown. Brief dated January 11, 1977. Contains some faint text and unclear resolution on specific closing reference ("Floyd").

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If Brown's law firm agrees to represent a physician in a license revocation hearing but no fees have been received by the firm, can the physician be a source of income to Brown?

CONCLUSION: The staff concluded at a meeting on January 10, 1977, that the physician could be a source of income to Brown if it were probable that Brown's pro rata share of the fees would total $250 or more. The rationale was that the income had been "promised" to the official once services had been agreed upon which would probably generate the requisite income.

The staff concluded at a meeting on January 10, 1977, that the physician could be a source of income to Brown if it were probable that Brown's pro rata share of the fees would total $250 or more. The rationale was that the income had been "promised" to the official once services had been agreed upon which would probably generate the requi- site income. I advised Floyd of these views on January 1 , 1977. TP: jo
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing whether a physician can be a source of income to an official when the official's law firm has agreed to represent the physician in a license revocation hearing but has not yet received fees. The FPPC staff concluded affirmatively if the official's pro rata share would probably total $250 or more.
